name: Czech B1 Vocabulary
description: Intermediate Czech — CEFR B1 level vocabulary

facts.explanation: působit — to work (to act as intended). Also: to work as, to be employed as, to cause, to act (to have an effect on)
pustit — to drop. Also: to let in, to release, to let go, to let go, to release, to set about, to start
Rakousko — Austria (a country in Central Europe).
rezervace — booking (A reservation for a service, such as accommodation in an hotel).
ruský — Russian (of or relating to Russia). Also: Russian (of or relating to Russian language)
setkat — to meet (to come face to face with someone by arrangement). Also: to meet (to converge and touch)
služba — service. Also: duty (period of time spent at work)
stadion — stadium (venue where sporting events are held).
stanice — station (place where a vehicle may stop).
těžce — heavily (in a manner designed for heavy duty).
trvat — to last (to endure, continue over time). Also: to insist
Ukrajina — Ukraine (a country in Eastern Europe, bordering on the north shore of the Black Sea).
ve skutečnosti — in fact (actually, in truth).
verze — version (specific form or variation of something). Also: version
vést — to lead (to guide). Also: to conduct (to transmit, as heat, light, electricity, etc.), to be doing, to do, to fare
vzít — to take. Also: to take someone somewhere, to take, to hold, to marry sb., to get married to sb.
zabít — to kill. Also: to kill (to express one’s anger at), to commit suicide, to get killed, to be killed
zachránit — to save (to help to survive).
zasedání — session (meeting of a council, court, or legislative body).
zastavit — to stop (to cease moving). Also: to pawn (put in a pawnshop), to stop, to halt, to pause
zdravý — healthy (enjoying health). Also: healthy (conducive to health)
znamenat — to mean (to have as a meaning).
znát — to know (to be acquainted or familiar with).
žít — to live. Also: to spend life (a certain way), to reside (permanently), to exist
bavit — to enjoy. Also: to entertain, to amuse (subject: person, object: person), to talk, to converse, to have fun
bojovat — to fight (to contend in physical conflict). Also: to fight (to strive for)
činnost — activity (state or quality of being active). Also: activity (something done)
dělat starosti — to worry (to be troubled).

divize — division (military unit usually consisting of between 10,000 and 30,000 soldiers). Also: division (group of teams who compete against each other for a championship), division (the operation of dividing a number by another)
dohromady — together. Also: taken together, combined
dosáhnout — to reach. Also: to reach, to achieve
heslo — password. Also: headword, motto, slogan, entry (article in a dictionary or encyclopedia)
hora — mountain. Also: a lot, tons
kampaň — campaign (series of operations undertaken to achieve a set goal, e.g. marketing, political, or military).
maminka — mum, mom (affectionate word for mother).
materiál — material (matter which may be shaped or manipulated).
ministr — minister (head of a government department).
mluvit — to speak. Also: to butt in
myslet — to think (to reckon, to estimate). Also: to think (to think about), to mean, to think, to guess, to suppose
naučit — to teach. Also: to learn
nedostatek — scarcity. Also: lack, deficiency, shortage, defect, deficiency
negativní — negative (not positive or neutral). Also: negative (undesirable), negative (relating to a negative in photography)
odpor — aversion. Also: resistance, resistor (electronic component)
pít — to drink (to take in liquid). Also: to drink (to take in alcoholic beverage)

počítat — to count. Also: to calculate, to number, to total, to count on, to expect, to take into account, to reckon, to assume, to expect, to count, to rank, to view [with accusative ‘someone/something’] [with k (+ dative) ‘as being among something’] or
pozitivní — positive (characterized by affirmation, constructiveness, or influence for the better; favourable).
praxe — practice (actual operation or experiment, in contrast to theory). Also: practicum (college course)
premiéra — premiere (of a play or performance). Also: opening night
provést — to guide (to serve as a guide person). Also: to implement
přenos — transfer. Also: transmission (act of transmitting)
přímý — straight (not crooked or bent). Also: direct
přinést — to bring (by foot an object).
ředitel — director (manager, not of film). Also: principal, headmaster, headteacher (the chief administrator of a school)
sloužit — to serve (somebody). Also: to serve as, to be used as
Slovensko — Slovakia (a country in Central Europe).
snížit — to lower (to reduce the height of).
spát — to sleep (to rest in a state of reduced consciousness). Also: to sleep (to have sex)
učit — to teach. Also: to learn
útočník — attacker (someone who attacks). Also: attacker, forward, forward, back
vzájemně — mutually (in the same way, each to the other).
vzor — paradigm. Also: model; example; pattern
vztah — relation (way in which two things may be associated).
web — the World Wide Web, the Internet. Also: web page
záležitost — business (something involving one personally). Also: matter
asistent — assistant (person who assists or helps someone else).
bránit — to defend. Also: to prevent
Černé moře — Black Sea (an inland sea bound by Southeastern Europe in the west, Eastern Europe in the north and northeast, and Turkey in the south).
dohoda — agreement. Also: deal (an agreement between parties), understanding (an informal contract, mutual agreement)
hlavní stránka — home page (the main or first page of a web site).
chovat — to behave. Also: to hold, to cradle
jinde — elsewhere (in or at some other place).
měnit — to change. Also: to replace, to exchange money
noční klub — nightclub (establishment that is open late at night).
obecní — municipal (related to a municipality). Also: municipal, communal (belonging to a municipality)
oběť — sacrifice. Also: victim, casualty (of war or accident)
odkaz — reference, cross-reference. Also: link, hyperlink, bequest
odstranit — to remove. Also: to eliminate (to completely destroy something so that it no longer exists)
podání ruky — handshake (grasping of hands by two people).
porazit — to defeat (to overcome). Also: to slaughter
poslouchat — to listen (to). Also: to obey
postava — character (being in a story). Also: figure (human figure; shape of human body)
postup — procedure (particular method for performing a task). Also: promotion (in rank), advancement, progress
pozornost — attention (mental focus). Also: favor (small gift)
pravidelně — regularly (with constant frequency or pattern).
princip — principle (rule of action). Also: principle (rule or law of nature)
přijmout — to accept. Also: to receive
rozhodnout — to decide (come to a resolution or judgment). Also: to decide, to decide, determine, settle
sledovat — to follow. Also: to tail, to track, to watch (object or event that changes state)
velitel — commander (one who exercises control and direction of a military or naval organization).
volit — to choose. Also: to elect
vrátit — to return. Also: to return, to go back
výběr — selection (process or act). Also: choice, withdrawal (taking out money from an account)
vytvořit — to create (to make sth new). Also: to create (to invent / to design sth)
agent — agent, spy (someone who works for an intelligence agency). Also: agent, representative (someone who represents a company)
být na řadě — to be next.
držet hubu — to shut up (to stop talking).
finanční úřad — tax office (government-established place to discuss tax affairs).
hovno — , turd (solid excretory product). Also: nothing
hřiště — playground. Also: field, playing field
individuální — individual (intended for a single person).
kontrola — inspection (the act of examining something, often closely). Also: feature (in orienteering)
lístek — small leaf. Also: ticket (admission to entertainment), ticket (pass for transportation)
Milan — a male given name, equivalent to English Milan. Also: beer belly, beer gut
mimochodem — by the way. Also: en passant
obor — discipline (specific branch of knowledge or learning).
ovládat — to control. Also: to control oneself, to dominate (to govern, rule or control by superior authority or power), to know, to master (to learn to a high degree of proficiency)
parlament — parliament. Also: parliament (building of a parliament)
pozadí — background (part of picture). Also: background (relevant information)
přechod — transition (process of change). Also: checkpoint, gradient, menopause, crossing
připravit — to prepare. Also: to get ready
přítelkyně — girlfriend (female partner in a romantic relationship). Also: girl friend (female friend)
role — role, part (of an actor). Also: lines (spoken text of an actor playing a part), role (e.g. of a person in a society), role (function of a constituent in a clause), scroll
řídit — to control. Also: to drive (a car), to steer (a vehicle, including ship), to govern, to follow (object: rules, instructions)
technika — technology (body of tools). Also: technique (way of accomplishing a task)
věřit — to believe. Also: to trust
vole — crop, craw (pouch-like part of the alimentary tract of some birds). Also: goitre
výraz — expression (facial appearance usually associated with an emotion). Also: expression, term
vyzkoušet — to examine (to test skills or qualifications of someone). Also: to try
základy — foundation. Also: elements (basic tenets of an area of knowledge)
závěr — conclusion. Also: cadence (chord progression which concludes a piece of music, section or musical phrases within it)
zlepšit — to improve (something). Also: to improve, to become better

aplikace — application (the act of applying as a means; the employment of means to accomplish an end). Also: application (a computer program or the set of software that the end user perceives as a single entity as a tool for a well-defined purpose)
asociace — association (a group of persons associated for a common purpose; an organization; society). Also: association (a connection to or an affiliation with something)
forma — form, shape. Also: mold/mould (for shaping a fluid or plastic substance)
instituce — institution (established organisation). Also: institution (custom or practice of a society or community such as marriage)
každopádně — in any case, in any event, either way.
letní čas — summer time, daylight-saving time.
mít dlouhou chvíli — to be bored.
Nová Anglie — New England (a geographic region of the northeastern United States, consisting of Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island and Vermont).
pohlaví — sex, gender (typically one of the categories male or female). Also: sex organs, genitals
pokračovat — to continue (to prolong). Also: to continue (to resume)
pošta — post office. Also: mail (parcels and letters)
prakticky — in practice. Also: virtually, basically, almost, practically
složení — composition. Also: ingredients (on a food label)
stáhnout — to withdraw. Also: to withdraw, to retreat, to contract, to download (to a local computer), to pull off, strip off, to skin, flay
střední škola — high school, secondary school.
teplota — temperature (measure of cold or heat). Also: low-grade fever, temperature (elevated body temperature)
věnovat — to dedicate. Also: to donate, to devote
vodní hodiny — water clock (device for measuring time).
životní prostředí — environment (natural world or ecosystem).
domácí vězení — house arrest (confinement of a person, by authorities, to his or her residence).
chybět — to be absent. Also: to be missed and longed for
jít spát — to go to bed.
mít plné zuby — to be fed up.
poznat — to recognize. Also: to experience
představit — to introduce (someone to someone else). Also: to introduce oneself, to imagine
příspěvek — contribution. Also: subscription, fee, due
režim — regime, mode (mode of rule or management). Also: regime (form of government)

facts.quizQuestion: What does "růst jako z vody" mean?
facts.explanation: růst jako z vody — to grow fast.
skutečný — real (having physical existence), actual (existing in act or reality, not just potentially).
stroj času — time machine (device used to travel in time).
šance — chance, odds, opportunity or possibility.
Východní Německo — East Germany (a former Communist country of Central Europe, existing between 1949 and 1990).
Západní Německo — West Germany (a former country in Central Europe, distinguished from the German Democratic Republic, commonly known as East Germany).
cíl o sobě — end in itself.

facts.quizQuestion: What does "cíl sám o sobě" mean?
facts.explanation: cíl sám o sobě — end in itself.
dávat smysl — to make sense (to be coherent or reasonable).
herec — actor, player (person who performs in a theatrical play or film).
jistý — certain, sure. Also: certain (having been determined but unspecified)
kvalifikace — qualification (racing). Also: qualification (for a job)
podat — to hand, give. Also: to submit, hand in (a request, etc.), to give, to serve, to administer (object: medicine)
postavit — to stand, put. Also: to stand up, set upright, to build, to stand up, to defy, oppose, to put (a question etc in a specific manner)
potraviny — grocery store. Also: food, foodstuffs, groceries
Spojené státy — United States (a country in North America).
správce — administrator. Also: manager (e.g. of an apartment building)
účast — participation. Also: attendance (state of attending)

Velká Británie — Great Britain (a large island (sometimes also including some of the surrounding smaller islands) off the north-west coast of Western Europe, made up of England, Scotland, and Wales). Also: Great Britain (the United Kingdom, a kingdom and country in Northern Europe including the island of Great Britain as well as Northern Ireland on the northeastern portion of the island of Ireland)
živý — alive, living. Also: lively, vivid, vivacious, animated
Česká republika — Czech Republic (a country in Central Europe).
deník — diary, journal. Also: journal, newspaper

jarní — spring, vernal (related to the season). Also: vernal (vernal point; the one of the two points on the celestial sphere where the celestial equator and ecliptic intersect, through which the Sun passes between 19 and 21 March)
jinak řečeno — in other words, put differently.
jinými slovy — in other words, put differently.
jít z kopce — to go downhill.
král zvířat — king of beasts (the lion).
místo narození — place of birth, birthplace.
národ — people, nation. Also: ethnic group, ethnicity
nechat na pokoji — to leave alone.
nejdříve — soonest, first, superlative degree of brzy.
policejní stanice — police station (building of police force).
rychlost světla — speed of light (the speed of electromagnetic radiation in a perfect vacuum).
Spojené království — United Kingdom (a kingdom and country in Northern Europe).
stačit — to suffice, do (work). Also: to cope
střední Evropa — Central Europe (a geographic region in the center of Europe, usually including Austria, Switzerland, the Czech Republic, Hungary, Poland, Slovakia, Slovenia, Croatia and Germany).
světová strana — cardinal point, cardinal direction.
televize — television, TV. Also: television set, TV set
trenér — coach, trainer (person who trains another).
ústavní — constitutional (relating to a constitution). Also: constitutional (conforming to)
ústí — mouth, estuary (the end of a river out of which water flows). Also: muzzle (the mouth or the end for entrance or discharge of a gun, pistol etc., that the bullet emerges from)
večeře — dinner, supper, evening meal (whether it is the main meal or not).

východní Evropa — Eastern Europe (a socio-political geographical area of eastern Europe usually including the European countries to the east of Germany, Austria and Italy, and to the west of the Urals).
z ničeho nic — out of nowhere.
základní škola — primary school (UK), elementary school (US); typically lasting from first through ninth grade.
západní Evropa — Western Europe (a sociopolitical region in the west of Europe).
cizí země — foreign country (country of which one is not a citizen).
čistě — cleanly, neatly. Also: purely, strictly, entirely
dříve či později — sooner or later.
dříve nebo později — sooner or later.
jižní Evropa — Southern Europe (a sociopolitical region of Europe including such countries as Spain, Portugal, Italy, and Greece).
kraj — edge, periphery, outskirts. Also: region
ministryně — female minister (head of a government department).
mít na paměti — to keep in mind, to bear in mind.
nahlas — aloud, out loud (using one's voice). Also: loudly (at a high volume)
osobní vlak — passenger train. Also: local train
prospěch — profit, benefit. Also: school results, grades
severní Evropa — Northern Europe (a sociopolitical region of Europe including such countries as Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden, Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania).
volno — leave, time off (from work). Also: spare time, leisure (time)

zbytek — rest, remainder. Also: remainder (in mathematics, what remains after repeatedly subtracting the divisor from the dividend), remainder (in commerce, excessive stock items left unsold and subject to reduction in price)
zpátky — back, backwards (to a previous condition or place).
železniční stanice — railway station, train station, railroad station.
dávat pozor — to pay attention, to be careful.
dosavadní — as it was so far, as it was up to now.
dotaz — inquiry, enquiry (request for information). Also: query (databases)
fotbal — football (UK), soccer (US, Australia), association football.
Ivan — a male given name, equivalent to English Ivan. Also: Russian (person)
ministr zahraničí — foreign minister, foreign secretary, Secretary of State (US).
nelze — it is impossible (to do). Also: it can't (be done)
obchodní dům — department store (store containing many departments).
plán B — plan B, fallback, backup plan, contingency plan.
projít — to go for a walk. Also: to pass, go through
státní svátek — national holiday (statutory holiday enacted by a country to commemorate the country itself).
svátek — holiday, day off (including Sundays). Also: public holiday, name day
věnovat pozornost — to pay attention; heed.
zadní — back, rear, hind (near the rear).
doktor — doctor, physician. Also: doctor, PhD
na druhou stranu — on the other hand.

skončit — to finish (to come to an end), to end (quick and abrupt; does not emphasize the process of ending, more abrupt than ukončit). Also: to end up (to arrive at a destination, sometimes unexpectedly)
vrchol — peak, summit, top. Also: vertex, node (vertex or a leaf in a graph of a network)
závod — competition, race (contest). Also: business, enterprise, factory, plant

facts.quizQuestion: What does "znát jako své boty" mean?
facts.explanation: znát jako své boty — to know real well.
dojít — to arrive (at), to come. Also: to run out, to click
hokej — hockey, ice hockey. Also: hockey, field hockey
nižší střední třída — lower middle class.
odtud — from there, thence (from this source or origin).
originální — genuine, authentic, original. Also: novel
pořad — program (U.S.), programme (U.K.) (presentation that is broadcast on radio or television). Also: agenda (list of matters to be taken up, as at a meeting)
pustit z hlavy — to forget about it, to never mind.
tudíž — hence, as a result, therefore.
vyšší střední třída — upper middle class.

využít — to use, to exploit, to take advantage of [with accusative ‘something’; or with genitive ‘’] (higher register with genitive). Also: to employ, to make use of [with accusative ‘something’; or with genitive ‘’] (higher register with genitive)
zábava — fun, entertainment, amusement. Also: rural dancing event, talk, conversation
zůstat — to stay, to remain (to remain in a particular place).
akorát — just right, fitting. Also: only, just
aktuální — current, up-to-date. Also: open (of an offer)
autobus — bus, coach, omnibus. Also: woodlouse
cizí — strange, unfamiliar (not yet part of one's experience). Also: foreign
dovolit — to allow, to permit. Also: to afford, to be impudent, to take the liberty
hezký jako obrázek — pretty as a picture.
manželství — marriage, matrimony, wedlock (union of two or more people).
mít chuť — to feel like, fancy.
nahoru — up, upward, upwards (in the direction away from earth’s centre).
požár — fire, conflagration (destructive and undesirable occurrence of fire).
přemýšlet — to think, to ponder, to contemplate (to go over in one's head). Also: to think (to communicate to oneself in one's mind, to try to find a solution to a problem)
sekce — section, department, division. Also: dissection
dosud — until now, up to now, so far.
drobné — change, small change, coins (coins of lower value).
hodit — [with dative] to fit, to suit. Also: be appropriate, come in handy
kanál — sewer, drain, gutter. Also: canal, channel
německé karty — German playing cards, cards with German suits.
odejít — to go away, walk off.
podnik — business, enterprise. Also: undertaking, enterprise
považovat — to consider, to view, to deem, to find [with accusative ‘’] and.
udržet — to hold and not lose. Also: to keep, to maintain
vypadat — to appear, look like, seem.
ministerstvo školství — ministry of education, education ministry.
řešit — to deal with, address. Also: to discuss, to bother
řešit sám sebe — to be self-interested; to deal with, take care of or think especially about things related to oneself.
vyrazit — to set out, to depart. Also: to surge, to knock out, to smash
zrušit — to cancel, to abolish, to call off.
zvýšit — to increase, to raise, to up.
hledat — to search, to look for.
Marie — a female given name, equivalent to English Mary. Also: Mary (biblical figure)
přestat — to stop, desist, cease. Also: to quit, to give up
stavba — building, construction (process). Also: building, structure
ukázat — to point, to point out. Also: to show, to demonstrate, to prove, to turn out, to manifest
vydat — to publish (to issue a publication), to release. Also: to spend, to expend, to set off
Washington — Washington, Washington, D.C. (the capital city of the United States). Also: Washington, Washington State (a state in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States)
cíl — goal, objective, target. Also: target (butt or mark to shoot at), finish, destination
líto — synonym of léto: summer (hottest season).
moment síly — moment, moment of force.

New York — New York, New York City (the largest city in the state of New York, and the largest city in the United States, a metropolis extending into neighboring New Jersey in addition to Pennsylvania and Connecticut). Also: New York (a state of the United States)
případně — possibly, if applicable, if needed. Also: appropriately
vyměnit — to exchange, to replace. Also: to swap, to exchange
bát — to be afraid of, to fear.
červené — ellipsis of červené víno (“red wine”).
dětský — child, children, child's, children's. Also: childish
divný — strange, odd, surprising. Also: queer, weird, fishy, suspicious
hana — slur, insult, defamation. Also: blame
jindy — in or at some other time.
mobilní telefon — mobile phone, cell phone.
neustále — all the time, constantly, incessantly, continually.
obraz — picture, painting, image. Also: image (something mapped to by a function)
pořad jednání — agenda, order of the day.
přiznat — to admit, to acknowledge. Also: to give, to grant, to admit guilt, to plead guilty, to confess
Spojené státy americké — United States of America (a country in North America, also including Hawaii in Oceania; short form Spojené státy m inan pl or Amerika f; abbreviation USA).
te — Combined form of to + je.
televizní pořad — television show, TV show.
těšit — to please, to make happy. Also: [with accusative] to look forward to, to enjoy (the benefits of something)
vysoké učení technické — university of technology, technical university.
znamení — sign, signal, indication. Also: sign, symbol, sign, omen, sign
způsobit — to cause, to bring about, to bring to pass.
dolů — down, downward, downwards (from a high to a low position).
současně — in parallel, concurrently. Also: and, at the same time
tj. — initialism of to je: i.e..
zjistit — to find out, to ascertain.
č. p. — initialism of číslo popisné.
přijít — to reach a place by walking; to come (on foot). Also: to lose
zajímat — to interest [with accusative ‘someone/something’], to be interesting. Also: to be interested
líbit — to please [with dative ‘someone’] (idiomatically translated by English like with subject and object reversed).
použít — to use [with accusative ‘something’; or with genitive ‘’] (higher register with genitive).
používat — to use [with accusative ‘something’; or with genitive ‘’] (higher register with genitive).
MHD — initialism of Městská hromadná doprava.
jít na nervy — to get on someone's nerves, to irritate.
dočkat — to get to see or experience after a wait.
oběd — lunch, meal eaten in the middle of the day (whether it is the main meal or not).
požádat — to ask [with accusative ‘somebody’ and o (+ accusative) ‘for something’], to request.
dan — dan, master and teacher of judo, karate or other Japanese martial arts.
OSN — initialism of Organizace spojených národů: UN.
ČSSD — Česká strana sociálně demokratická: A Czech social democratic political party.
pí — pi (Greek letter). Also: pi (the ratio of the circumference of a Euclidean circle to its diameter)
vůl — ox (castrated male cattle). Also: idiot (stupid person), dude (a term of address for a person)
ar — are (unit of area equal to 100 square metres).
archa — ark (the ship built by Noah).

beran — ram (heavy device for battering down walls). Also: ram (heavy metal weight for driving piles), ram (device on a ship for ramming other ships), conical hay drying rack, garland of dogwood or yarrow branches for Palm Sunday, curved bicycle handlebars
částka — sum (quantity of money). Also: part
desítka — ten (digit or figure). Also: 10-degree beer (beer having 10% original extract), penalty kick
dvojka — two (digit or figure). Also: deuce (cards)

eso — ace (highest ranking card). Also: ace (a serve won without the opponent hitting the ball), ace (a point won directly from a serve), hole in one, flying ace (a fighter pilot credited with shooting down five or more enemy aircraft)
fanoušek — fan (admirer or aficionado, especially of a sport or performer; someone who is fond of something or someone).
kostka — die (polyhedron used in games of chance). Also: cube (object more or less in the form of a cube)
kráva — cow (female domesticated ox or other bovine, especially an adult after she has had a calf). Also: bitch (objectionable woman), big, large object
lůžko — bed. Also: A place, surface or layer on which something else rests.
paže — arm (portion of the upper limb).
policajt — cop (police officer or prison guard).
prase — pig. Also: pig (person who does disgusting things)
řádek — row (in a table). Also: row, line (single horizontal row of text on a screen or printed paper)
sada — set (collection of similar things). Also: set

sestřih — cut (the act of shortening by cutting; the result thereof). Also: haircut, cut (the style into which the hair is cut), cut, edit (a particular edited version of media, especially film or video footage)
schránka — box (a case, etc., in which something is stored).
slepice — hen (female chicken). Also: stupid woman
smutný — sad (in a sad state). Also: sad (causing sadness)
stáří — age (duration of an entity). Also: age (state of being old)

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
